wickets.name: Log4j loaded at runtime
wickets.description: Log4j classes are loaded during execution.

wickets.name: Outbound egress permits callback
wickets.description: Network policy permits outbound connectivity needed for remote resolution/callback.

wickets.name: Attacker-controlled input reaches a Log4j sink
wickets.description: Untrusted input is logged by Log4j on a reachable code path.

wickets.name: Exposure class supports attacker access
wickets.description: Service exposure posture allows attacker interaction (public/internal/post-auth).

wickets.name: JNDI lookup capability present
wickets.description: Required lookup capability/classes are present/active in runtime.

wickets.name: Lookups enabled in effective configuration
wickets.description: Effective logging configuration permits lookups / message substitution relevant to the path.

wickets.name: DNS resolution allowed
wickets.description: Environment permits DNS resolution required for outbound target discovery.

wickets.name: LDAP/RMI outbound permitted
wickets.description: Environment permits LDAP/RMI egress or equivalent protocol needed for classic JNDI retrieval.

wickets.name: HTTP/HTTPS outbound permitted
wickets.description: Environment permits HTTP(S) egress (alternative retrieval / callback).

wickets.name: Proxy-only outbound with allowlist
wickets.description: Outbound is constrained to proxy and allowlist governs destinations.

wickets.name: Process spawn permitted
wickets.description: Policy allows exec/spawn needed for high-impact payloads.

wickets.name: Relevant file write permitted
wickets.description: Policy allows writing to locations needed for persistence/impact.

wickets.name: Outbound channel lacks strong endpoint authentication
wickets.description: Outbound retrieval/callback channel can be intercepted/modified due to missing TLS validation, weak trust, or use of cleartext protocols.

wickets.name: No certificate pinning / proxy interception permitted
wickets.description: Client permits interception (e.g., enterprise proxy) and does not pin certificates or otherwise prevent MITM substitution.

attack_paths.name: Log4j Lookup/Pattern DoS v1
attack_paths.notes: Objective: resource exhaustion via expensive pattern/lookup evaluation; impact depends on rate limits and logging volume.

attack_paths.name: Log4j Lookup-Driven Info Disclosure v1
attack_paths.notes: Objective: attacker-controlled input triggers lookup evaluation that may disclose environment/runtime data via logs or error channels; avoids assuming outbound retrieval.

attack_paths.name: Log4j JNDI Callback (network interaction) v1
attack_paths.notes: Objective: external influence triggers outbound lookup/callback. Does not assert final impact.

attack_paths.name: Log4j JNDI RCE (generic) v1
attack_paths.notes: Minimal generic path: runtime load + reachable sink + access posture + capability + config + dns + egress.

attack_paths.name: Log4j Outbound Retrieval with MITM Injection v1
attack_paths.notes: Objective: outbound retrieval/callback is possible AND the channel can be intercepted/modified (MITM), increasing attacker control when traffic traverses untrusted networks or intercepting proxies.
